notes.sg-extinction-risk-climate-change-enviro: This is a specific type of ecological niche modeling, which is already covered by 'ecological niche'.
notes.sg-extinction-risk-climate-change-r-pack: This seems to be a tool or method rather than a core topic. Merging to 'model selection' as it relates to modeling.
notes.sg-extinction-risk-climate-change-ensemb: This is a specific type of forecasting of species distributions.
notes.sg-extinction-risk-climate-change-foreca: This is a more specific version of 'forecasting of species distributions'.
notes.sg-extinction-risk-climate-change-ensemb: This is a more specific version of 'forecasting of species distributions'.
notes.sg-climate-model-radiative-forcing: This is a type of cloud forcing.
notes.sg-climate-model-shortwave-radiation: This is a type of cloud forcing.
notes.sg-global-warming-of-global-warming: The label 'global warming' is too broad and could be confused with the parent topic itself. Relabeling to 'global warming impacts' makes it a more specific subtopic.
notes.sg-global-warming-scientific-consensus: This is a good subtopic related to the social and scientific aspects of global warming.
notes.sg-global-warming-ecological-forecasting: This is a specific and relevant subtopic concerning the effects of global warming on ecosystems.
notes.sg-global-warming-shutdown-of-thermohali: This is a specific and significant potential consequence of global warming.
notes.sg-global-warming-antarctic-sea-ice: This is a specific geographical area and component directly affected by global warming.
notes.sg-global-warming-ice-age: While ice ages are climate phenomena, they are typically long-term cycles and not direct subtopics of current 'global warming'. This might be too broad or a different timescale.
notes.sg-atmospheric-science-atmospheric-chemi: label is a bit short, expanded to match member terms
notes.sg-greenhouse-gas-greenhouse-gas-removal: This is a method for mitigating greenhouse gas emissions, not a direct cause or component of the greenhouse effect itself. It is better represented as a subtopic of climate change mitigation.
notes.sg-climate-change-permafrost-atmospheric: The parent topic is 'climate change permafrost', so 'permafrost carbon cycle' is more specific and relevant than 'atmospheric carbon cycle'.
